runs.review_conclusion: The historical fix only replaces an error branch with a loading branch. Me.ets and navigation parameter flow are plausibly causal, so the original expected-file and category oracle was too narrow.

quality_policy: suppress weak log anchors when no strong log identity activates path context
retain only relations connected to returned anchors
omit generated summaries that repeat file and symbol
surface evidence gaps instead of filling the payload with weak matches

case_deltas.review_note: The source conclusion and expected file were correct, but the Agent chose resource instead of the frozen media category. The oracle was not changed after observing the result.

case_results.review_note: All three Memory trials selected media. The earlier resource classification was a single-run variation.
Two trials included Index.ets as a supporting predicted file, reducing precision while retaining the expected ChatList.ets.

post_optimization_three_trial_run.conclusion: Partial cost improvement with a split-view predicted-file precision tradeoff; the original efficiency target was not met.

case_results.review_note: ChatList.ets remained causal while Index.ets moved to supporting_files.
One trial stopped on ChatDetail.ets without inspecting MessageBubble.ets because the compact query returned only generic chat anchors.

file_role_refinement_external_run.conclusion: File-role reporting improved navigation precision, but the batch failed because weak WebM anchor recall combined with an insufficient supported-cause stopping precondition.

case_results.review_note: All trials found ChatList.ets, but mechanism_evidence_files also included supporting Index.ets and failed the v2 evidence-role gate.
One trial labeled the correctly described parallel async mechanism as state; one trial also reported two expansion rounds with one reason code.
All three trials selected media and MessageBubble.ets using two primary anchors with no non-anchor expansion.

external_run.conclusion: The targeted WebM retrieval failure was resolved, but the batch remains non-promotable because login category stability and v2 evidence-role/budget reporting failed.

observation_failures: login trial 3 inspected Login/Index.ets without recording it in expansion_trace
WebM trial 2 used four source searches against a budget of three
WebM Memory trials 2 and 3 classified the media-loading failure as api
post_run_corrections: derive repeated reason codes directly from expansion_trace without deduplication
align the total-file cap with three primaries plus two two-file expansion rounds
prefer media for media loading, decoding, playback, and local resource access even when an API is misused

quality_gate_failures: navigation memory trial 1 read 4 times across 3 files
navigation memory trial 2 read 6 times across 3 files
login memory trial 1 opened no source and used agent-reported zero-search telemetry
login memory trial 2 omitted Login/Index.ets from expansion_trace
WebM memory trial 2 read 7 times across 2 files
WebM memory trial 3 read 5 times across 2 files
login memory trial 1 traced 3 of 4 expanded files
login memory trial 2 traced 2 of 4 expanded files and exceeded the one-round two-file expansion budget
login memory trial 3 traced 3 of 4 expanded files

interpretation_limits: The pinned sample contains only three development pairs.
Earlier exploratory pairs used the default unpinned model.
Query rounds are reported by the external Agent.
Source-search counts before v4 are Agent-reported; v4 Codex runs require JSONL command telemetry.
Token usage comes from Codex CLI usage events.
No HarmonyOS runtime reproduction or focused project test was available.
Read-only source diagnosis cannot claim verification pass or verified causality.
Compact payload reduction, one single-trial A/B, and pre/post three-trial A/B runs were completed.
Aggregate non-regression cannot mask a regressed individual case.
The repeated run shows stable quality uplift on three development cases, not general Memory uplift.
Post-optimization Memory still used more total model tokens, elapsed time, and inspected source files than its same-batch Baseline.
The before/after cost reduction is small and accompanied by lower predicted-file precision.
The file-role-refinement batch failed two quality gates because one WebM Memory trial stopped on an irrelevant expansion anchor.
The v2 external batch resolved the targeted WebM failure but failed login per-case non-regression and source-exploration contract gates.
The v3 external batch resolved login category stability but regressed WebM category stability and violated two exploration observations, so it is not promotable.
The v4 external development batch passed every gate with perfect per-case stability and Runner-telemetry search counts.
V4 remains a three-case development result, not a Holdout or general-uplift claim; model-token and elapsed-time overhead remain.
The immutable v4 pack predates detailed cost fields; the separate v4 attribution batch provides complete aggregate cost telemetry without persisting reasoning or tool output.

The attribution batch passed quality but failed efficiency: cumulative Token overhead is dominated by cached-input replay, while uncached input, source-search count, source-read count, and command-output bytes decreased.
One Baseline WebM trial opened no source and is a quality/cost outlier; excluding its pair still leaves material Memory input and elapsed overhead.
Non-zero command exits are review signals because privacy-safe aggregate telemetry cannot distinguish expected no-match searches from real tool failures in the v4 artifact.
The v5 external batch produced perfect diagnosis/file scores but failed two source-search-budget observations and narrowly missed both efficiency limits, so it is not promotable.
V5 reduced commands and source-read volume substantially; its next candidate must restore explicit pre-command search/read ledgers without relaxing quality or efficiency gates.
V6 restored search/read ledgers and added a one-read quality audit; its completed external A/B failed quality and efficiency.
The v6 external batch eliminated search-budget violations but failed repeated-read, trace, one no-source quality observation, and both efficiency limits; it is not promotable.
V6 error-family telemetry attributed all Memory non-zero exits to other commands, not search misses/errors or source-read errors, without persisting command content.
V7 retains the audited search ledger, records complete tool-less turns as runner-measured zero searches, and evaluates bounded repeated reads through efficiency rather than diagnosis quality.
The v7 external batch achieved perfect diagnosis and file scores but failed all three login expansion traces plus Token and elapsed limits, so it is not promotable.
V7 aggregate read amplification passed while the WebM case reached 2.8333, showing that aggregate efficiency can mask a single-case regression.
V8 derives expansion accounting from investigated files and primary anchors, and requires every case to pass Token, elapsed, search, and read-efficiency checks in addition to aggregate checks.
V8 remains a local candidate pending repeated external A/B; historical v4-v7 quality outcomes remain unchanged under compatibility replay.
